description: Per-state, per-cycle election-data URLs from state Secretary-of-State / election-board websites. Used by scrape_state_sos.py to fetch primary-election results for the post-Callais Gingles 2/3 use case (within-party racial-bloc-voting analysis). URLs and formats change per cycle — this file is the local cache. Each entry is unverified until a contributor confirms the URL is live and the format parses; see notes per state.

LA._notes: Louisiana publishes parish-level (= county-level) primary results via the voterportal. Precinct-level data is generally available per parish but not as a single statewide file. This is the Callais home state — primary-election bloc-voting analysis here is the headline §2 use case. URLs change per election; verify before running.

2020-presidential-primary.url: TODO: verify at sos.la.gov for the 2020 presidential primary results page

2024-presidential-primary.url: TODO: verify at sos.la.gov for the 2024 presidential primary

AL._notes: Alabama publishes county-level certified summaries; precinct-level data must be requested from individual probate judges' offices. Allen v. Milligan baseline — the demographic pattern that drove the Allen majority opinion. Limited automated access; OpenElections coverage may be better.

2020-presidential-primary.url: TODO: verify; Alabama publishes results as PDFs typically
2020-presidential-primary.warning: PDF format may require manual extraction. Consider OpenElections instead.

GA._notes: Georgia uses Clarity Elections (results.enr.clarityelections.com/GA) which publishes XML and JSON downloads alongside the web portal. This is the most automation-friendly of the three. Each cycle is a separate Clarity URL with a numeric election ID.

2020-presidential-primary.url: TODO: verify Clarity election ID for GA 2020 primary
